# Oncoteam

> When someone you love has cancer, you become their advocate. Oncoteam is your AI ally — it helps you understand lab results, ask the right questions, find clinical trials across Europe and the US, and make sure nothing gets overlooked. All from your phone.

## The Story

Oncoteam was built by Peter Fusek — an entrepreneur and technology enthusiast, not a doctor — when his wife Erika was diagnosed with metastatic colorectal cancer. Buried in lab results, genetic reports, and treatment protocols he didn't understand, he built what he needed: a tool to keep up with a situation that moves faster than any one person can track.

Alongside Oncoteam (the intelligence layer), he built [Oncofiles](https://github.com/peter-fusek/oncofiles) — the data layer that organizes all medical documents from Google Drive. Oncofiles structures and connects them. Oncoteam turns them into understanding, questions, and action.

An [Instarea](https://www.instarea.com) project.

## Who It's For

- Cancer patients who want to understand and track their own treatment
- Family members acting as patient advocates — spouses, children, siblings
- Care providers coordinating between doctors, family, and the patient
- Anyone who needs a second opinion backed by medical textbooks and the latest research — not to replace the doctor, but because even the best oncologist has hundreds of patients and fifteen minutes per visit

## What Oncoteam Does

- Explains lab results in plain language with trend charts, safety thresholds, and cycle-over-cycle comparisons
- Prepares personalized questions before every oncologist visit based on your latest data
- Searches clinical trials from ClinicalTrials.gov and EU registries across 14 European countries, matched to your molecular profile
- AI-powered trial funnel: classifies each trial into excluded / later line / watching / action needed
- Cross-references your treatment with medical textbooks (DeVita), NCCN protocols, and latest PubMed research
- Tracks treatment trends — tumor markers, blood counts, inflammation indices — so you can see if treatment is working
- Pre-cycle safety checks with automated hold/go recommendations
- Sends WhatsApp alerts for safety issues, weekly briefings, and trial updates
- Provides bilingual interface (Slovak/English) with medical and plain-language modes
- Generates family-friendly treatment summaries shareable via WhatsApp or print
- WhatsApp onboarding: 8-step state machine with media attachments and admin approval
- 18 autonomous AI agents for monitoring, research, and reporting
- Stores all patient data in your own Google Drive (GDPR compliant)

## Stats

- 18 AI agents working autonomously per patient
- 50+ tasks per patient per week (briefings, trial searches, lab checks, alerts)
- 500+ clinical trials monitored across US and EU registries
- 6 specialist roles (lab analyst, trial researcher, protocol checker, briefing writer, family communicator, appointment prep)
- 24 MCP tools, 21 dashboard API routes
- 620 passing tests, ~10,700 lines of Python
- Multi-patient support with per-patient scheduling and OAuth

## Architecture

- Built on the Model Context Protocol (MCP) — open standard for AI tool integration
- Two-layer architecture: Oncofiles (data/persistence) + Oncoteam (intelligence)
- Patient data stored in patient-owned Google Drive — no local database
- Dashboard built with Nuxt 4 (SSR), backend with Python 3.12 / FastMCP 3.0+
- Deployed on Railway with zero-downtime deploys
- Circuit breaker + semaphore concurrency control for resilience
- Free tier: 200 documents, 500 AI queries/month, 100 agent runs/month

## Integrations

- Claude AI (autonomous agent loop, deep analysis)
- PubMed (NCBI E-utilities)
- ClinicalTrials.gov (API v2)
- EU Clinical Trials registries
- WhatsApp (Twilio)
- Google Drive (via Oncofiles)
- Model Context Protocol (MCP)

## Access

- **Website**: https://oncoteam.cloud
- **Dashboard**: https://dashboard.oncoteam.cloud
- **WhatsApp**: Chat anytime for quick answers, summaries, and alerts
- **Claude.ai**: Deep analysis and Q&A via MCP connector
